title: Hosting app utilizing socket.io on monday code hosting platform?

posts.author: orensayag (oren)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

we’re developing a monday.com app and we want it to utilize socket.io for client-server communication.

Is using web socket connections between client and server sides in a monday.com app possible given we’re hosting it in the monday code hosting platform?

Thanks, Oren

posts.author: kylecordes (Kyle Cordes)
posts.cookedHtml:
I do not work at monday, but I believe I know the answer here. monday code runs on Google Cloud Run.

So the answer is that it probably will work if you follow the practices as described in this Google Cloud Run documentation page.

Google Cloud

Using WebSockets  |  Cloud Run Documentation  |  Google...

posts.author: Semivies (Semivies)
posts.cookedHtml: I had a similar issue with WebSocket support and found that having a separate hosting setup made things smoother. I used https://www.librecloud.host/nextcloud-hosting/ to spin up a Nextcloud server and ran my socket server from there. Kept the logic off the Monday code environment and just connected via APIs. It’s been reliable so far and lets you keep more control over the backend stuff.
